curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: Stand-up is just jazz for people who never learned an instrument — you're flying blind at a hundred miles an hour, praying your mouth knows something your brain doesn't. And the SECOND it works, some jackass in row three yells 'DO THE VOICE!' Sir. I am having a breakthrough up here, and you want the duck.
quotes.speaker: Robin Williams
quotes.context: On his comedy process, Inside the Actors Studio, 2001
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the manic free-association, the swerve into a character, the 'do the voice' heckle: that's the Williams whirlwind. The tell — the real him would've spun into three more voices before the sentence ended; this one stays suspiciously on a single track.

quotes.text: The strangest thing I've tried to snort? My father. I snorted my father. He was cremated and I couldn't resist grinding him up with a little bit of blow. My dad wouldn't have cared, he didn't give a shit. It went down pretty well, and I'm still alive.
quotes.speaker: Keith Richards
quotes.context: Rolling Stones guitarist Keith Richards, in an April 2007 NME interview, describing the strangest thing he had snorted — his late father's cremated ashes ground up with cocaine. Reported by NME, The Hollywood Reporter, VOA, and others. Richards' manager later told NME the remark was made in jest, and Richards offered a softened account in his 2010 memoir, but he is documented giving this quote in the interview.

quotes.text: I blew $20 million. I snorted my Porsche. I snorted my plane. I snorted my house in that din of drugs and booze and being lost.
quotes.speaker: Steven Tyler
quotes.context: Tallying up what his cocaine habit cost him, in his 2011 rock memoir "Does the Noise in My Head Bother You?"

quotes.text: Marriage counseling! You pay a guy a hundred bucks an hour to tell you what your wife's been screaming AT you, for FREE, for a DECADE! OH! OHHHH! You want her to open up?! She's OPEN, pal! The whole CUL-DE-SAC's got the minutes!
quotes.speaker: Sam Kinison
quotes.context: His marriage routine during a 1988 set at Dangerfield's, New York
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the trademark scream, the marital rage, the 'free for a decade' math: that's the whole Kinison act in four sentences. The tell: his real screamers usually detonated off a nastier, darker setup; this one's almost good-natured by his standards.

quotes.text: Women say they want honesty. No you don't. You want a flattering lie said with a straight face and a lot of confidence. I told a woman the actual truth one time and she's still mad — and she should be! It was unflattering AND correct. That's the worst combination there is.
quotes.speaker: Patrice O'Neal
quotes.context: On relationships during a 2011 appearance on The Black Phillip Show podcast
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the blunt, provocative 'truth-bomb' framing about men and women is dead-center in Patrice's lane. The tell: the real him would've pushed it somewhere far more uncomfortable; this one pulls the punch right at the end.

quotes.text: Little Miss Muffet sat on a tuffet, eating her curds and whey. Along came a spider, he sat down beside her, he said, "Hey, what's in the bowl, bitch?"
quotes.speaker: Andrew Dice Clay
quotes.context: A signature filthy nursery-rhyme parody from his late-'80s/early-'90s arena act; crowds would shout the punchlines back. Popularized on his stand-up specials and documented in VICE's piece on his nursery rhymes.
